title: target os  mismatch with current os linux on fedora 26
body:
```
# ./buildah containers
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
b5fd548af695     *     b718692c239f docker.io/library/fedora:latest  fedora-working-container
cb7eae36fffb     *     d12c4c5322b2 docker.io/library/fedora:26      fedora-working-container-2
6a01b175a994     *     5a584b3d508d docker.io/library/busybox:latest busybox-working-container
[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah run fedora-working-container ls
target os  mismatch with current os linux
[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah run fedora-working-container-2 ls
target os  mismatch with current os linux
[root@fedora buildah]# ./buildah run busybox-working-container ls
target os  mismatch with current os linux
```

comments.body:
This could be a runc issue, with buildah using a different version of runc then you have installed. Now that we have runc v1.0 released this should not be as much of an issue.

What OS and what version of runc and buildah are you using?
Tested with latest runc is ok.
